vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1055

plan.title: triage:bootstrap / cache:fetch-all / scm:issue:list cannot filter ingest by author

narratives.Description: triage:bootstrap / cache:fetch-all / scm:issue:list cannot filter ingest by author
narratives.Overview:
## Symptom

`task triage:bootstrap` and the underlying `task cache:fetch-all` / `task scm:issue:list` surface have **no `--author` filter**. Operators who want to ingest only a subset of a backlog scoped to a specific author (e.g. only issues filed by `dependabot[bot]`, only issues opened by a specific maintainer during a triage sweep, or only the bot-authored noise to drain before a human pass) cannot do it through the canonical surface; they must drop down to ad-hoc `gh api repos/.../issues?creator=...` loops driven by `task cache:put` per-issue.

This is the `--author` companion to #1033 (`--label` filter gap). Same plumbing surfaces, same recurrence pattern, same workaround shape — different facet of the same intake-layer limitation.

## Reproduction

On master @ HEAD (deftai/directive), 2026-05-11:

```pwsh
task triage:bootstrap -- --repo deftai/directive --help
# argparse surface: --project-root / --repo / --limit / --state / --batch-size /
#                   --delay-ms / --fetch-timeout-s / --quiet / --json
# (post-#1033 may also expose --label; still no --author / --creator / --assignee.)

task cache:fetch-all -- --source github-issue --repo deftai/directive --help
# Same: no author filter; only --batch-size / --delay-ms / --ttl-seconds / --state / --limit.
```

Confirm by reading the source:

- `scripts/triage_bootstrap.py::_build_parser` — argparse surface does not expose `--author`.
- `scripts/cache.py::_cmd_fetch_all` → `cache_fetch_all` — forwards only `state` / `limit` (and post-#1033, `labels`) to `_cache_fetch.run_fetch_all`.
- `scripts/_cache_fetch.py::scm_list_issues` — invokes `task scm:issue:list -- --repo OWNER/REPO --state STATE --limit N --json number,title,state,updatedAt`. The `gh issue list` / REST `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues` wrapper underneath natively accepts `--author <user>` (gh) / `?creator=<login>` (REST), but the wrapper does not expose it.

Ad-hoc workaround (matches the #1033 shape):

```pwsh
$repo = "deftai/directive"
$author = "dependabot[bot]"
$nums = gh api "repos/$repo/issues?creator=$author&state=open&per_page=100" `
        --jq '[.[] | select(.pull_request == null) | .number] | sort' | ConvertFrom-Json
foreach ($n in $nums) {
    $tmp = [System.IO.Path]::GetTempFileName()
    gh api "repos/$repo/issues/$n" > $tmp
    task cache:put -- github-issue "$repo/$n" --raw-file $tmp
    Remove-Item $tmp
}
```

This works but bypasses the orchestrator entirely — no rate-limit handling, no 429 retry, no TTL skip-fresh idempotency, no batch / delay knobs, no recap.

## Why this matters

- Author-scoped cohorts are a real triage primitive: bot-authored backlog (dependabot, renovate, codeowners-bot) is best drained as a batch before a human-author pass; conversely, a maintainer reviewing their own filed issues wants the inverse cohort. Without `--author`, both paths force operators into ad-hoc REST loops.
- `--author` and `--label` compose naturally — "all `bug`-labelled issues filed by `dependabot[bot]`" is one of the most common real-world intake filters and is trivial at the `gh`/REST layer but unreachable through the orchestrator.
- The REST surface `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues` exposes three related facets: `creator`, `assignee`, `mentioned`. `--author` is the highest-value one; `--assignee` is the second (cohort triage of an individual maintainer's queue).
- Same operational argument as #1033: large backlogs (1000+ issues) force operators into all-or-nothing runs; author-scoped subsets are the natural unit for parallel triage workers.

## Recommended remediation paths

1. **Plumb `--author` through the four layers (mirror #1033):**
   - `scripts/scm.py issue list` accepts `--author LOGIN` → forwards to `gh issue list --author LOGIN` (and `?creator=LOGIN` for `--rest`).
   - `_cache_fetch.scm_list_issues(repo, state, limit, labels=None, author=None)` forwards the flag.
   - `cache_fetch_all(..., author=None)` accepts and passes through.
   - `scripts/triage_bootstrap.py::_build_parser` exposes `--author` forwarded via the same plumbing path the existing `--limit` / `--state` (and post-#1033, `--label`) use.

2. **Bundle the related REST facets while touching the plumbing.** Once `--author` lands, `--assignee` and `--mentioned` are one-line additions on each layer. Filing them together avoids three round-trips through the same four files.

3. **Compose with `--label` from #1033.** The plumbing change in #1033 should be the template for this one — same argparse pattern, same forwarder signature growth, same orchestrator pass-through. If #1033 has not landed yet, fold `--author` into the same PR; if it has, this issue is a strict follow-up with the same shape.

4. **Alternative: `--candidate-set` file input** (already proposed as Story B on #1033). Covers `--author` for free since the operator pre-computes the cohort. Still worth exposing `--author` directly for ergonomics — the candidate-set path is general-purpose escape-hatch, not a daily-driver surface.

## Suggested scope split

- Story A — `--author` plumbing through the four layers. Single PR. Mirrors #1033 Story A.
- Story B — `--assignee` plumbing on the same four layers (one-line per layer once Story A lands).
- Story C — docs surface documenting `--author` + `--label` + `--state` composition (where to use which, how they intersect, common cohort recipes).

## Refs

- #1033 — `--label` filter gap (this issue is the `--author` companion; same plumbing surfaces, same workaround shape)
- Session 2026-05-11 surfaced the gap when planning an author-scoped triage drain
- `scripts/triage_bootstrap.py`
- `scripts/cache.py::cache_fetch_all`
- `scripts/_cache_fetch.py::scm_list_issues`
- `tasks/scm.yml::issue:list`
- GitHub REST `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues` — `creator`, `assignee`, `mentioned` query params
- `gh issue list --author` — native CLI surface already wraps the REST `creator` facet

references.title: Issue #1055: triage:bootstrap / cache:fetch-all / scm:issue:list cannot filter ingest by author
